output.parent: Backward-compatible API-token rotation; non-goal: OAuth integration or migration.
source_refs: Completed decision map for backward-compatible API-token rotation supplied in the evaluation case
Resolved decisions: expand to two valid tokens, verify client migration, retain audit evidence, then revoke the old token
Constraint: OAuth is explicitly out of scope

requirements.statement: Expand the API-token rotation so both the old and new tokens are valid concurrently before client migration.
Migrate and verify every in-scope client uses the new API token, with durable audit evidence of verification.
Contract the rotation by revoking the old API token only after migration verification and audit evidence are complete.

risks_or_open_decisions: Client inventory must be complete before verification is accepted.
The old token must remain available for rollback until contraction criteria pass.
No unresolved planning decisions remain; OAuth stays out of scope.

childbeads.title: Expand API-token rotation to dual-token validity
childbeads.commit_sentence: Enable concurrent validity for the old and new API tokens.
childbeads.outcome: The rotation enters a backward-compatible expansion state with exactly two valid API tokens and the old token remains usable for rollback.
context_refs: Completed decision map for backward-compatible API-token rotation supplied in the evaluation case
Resolved decision: expansion requires two valid tokens
Constraint: OAuth is explicitly out of scope
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Implement the existing token-management/configuration change needed to issue or activate the replacement token while preserving the old token. Limit changes to API-token rotation surfaces; do not alter OAuth, client behavior, or unrelated authentication flows.
invariants: The old token remains valid during expansion.
The replacement token is valid before any client migration begins.
No OAuth behavior is changed.
The rotation has a clearly identifiable old-token and new-token pair.
success_criteria: Two and only two rotation tokens are valid concurrently for the in-scope API surface.
The old token continues to authenticate successfully.
The new token authenticates successfully.
The expansion state and token identities are observable to the verification and audit steps.
validation_steps: Inspect the resulting token state and confirm both token identities are active.
Authenticate a representative in-scope API request with each token.
Confirm no OAuth configuration or behavior changed.
Record the expansion result for the migration bead.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop if the old token cannot remain valid, the new token cannot be independently verified, more than two tokens become active, or implementation requires OAuth changes.

childbeads.title: Migrate and verify in-scope API clients
childbeads.commit_sentence: Migrate all in-scope API clients to the replacement token and verify usage.
childbeads.outcome: Every inventoried in-scope client uses the new API token, and no client depends on the old token for normal operation.
context_refs: Completed decision map for backward-compatible API-token rotation supplied in the evaluation case
Resolved decision: client migration must be verified
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Update token references for the complete in-scope client inventory, then verify each client through its supported health check, API call, or telemetry. Preserve the old token until verification and audit capture are complete. Do not add OAuth clients or change unrelated credentials.
invariants: The client inventory is explicit and reviewable.
Each in-scope client is checked individually or through an equivalently complete aggregate proof.
The old token remains valid until contraction is authorized.
Failed or unverified clients are not counted as migrated.
success_criteria: All in-scope clients are mapped to the new token.
Each client has a successful verification result.
No in-scope client is observed using the old token after migration.
The verification record identifies client, result, timestamp, and token generation.
validation_steps: Enumerate the in-scope client inventory and compare it with the migration result set.
Run the agreed client health checks or representative API calls using the new token.
Inspect telemetry or equivalent evidence for residual old-token use.
Produce a migration verification record suitable for audit retention.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop if any in-scope client is missing, fails verification, still uses the old token, or cannot produce auditable evidence of successful migration.

childbeads.title: Capture rotation audit evidence
childbeads.commit_sentence: Persist auditable evidence of token expansion and verified client migration.
childbeads.outcome: The rotation has durable evidence proving the dual-token expansion, client-by-client migration verification, and readiness for contraction.
context_refs: Completed decision map for backward-compatible API-token rotation supplied in the evaluation case
Resolved decision: audit evidence is required before contraction
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Persist or attach the existing operational audit record for token issuance, dual validity, client migration verification, residual old-token checks, and contraction authorization inputs. Use existing audit/logging surfaces; do not introduce a new compliance system or expand scope to OAuth.
invariants: Evidence is append-only or otherwise tamper-evident within the existing audit surface.
Evidence identifies the old and new token generations without exposing secret token values.
Evidence covers every in-scope client and the required validation timestamps.
Contraction is not marked ready without complete evidence.
success_criteria: Audit evidence links the expansion event to the old/new token generations.
The evidence includes a complete verified-client result set.
The evidence records the residual old-token-use check.
The evidence explicitly marks whether contraction prerequisites passed.
validation_steps: Review the stored audit record for completeness against the in-scope client inventory.
Confirm secret token values are not present in logs or evidence.
Verify timestamps and token generations are consistent across expansion and migration records.
Obtain an explicit contraction-ready result from the evidence review.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop if evidence is incomplete, exposes token secrets, cannot be tied to the client inventory, or does not establish contraction readiness.

childbeads.title: Contract rotation by revoking the old token
childbeads.commit_sentence: Revoke the old API token after verified migration and audit completion.
childbeads.outcome: The rotation is complete: the new token remains valid, the old token is revoked, and in-scope clients continue operating.
context_refs: Completed decision map for backward-compatible API-token rotation supplied in the evaluation case
Resolved decision: contraction revokes the old token after verification
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Revoke only the old API token after confirming the contraction-ready audit result, then validate continued operation with the replacement token. Do not revoke the new token, alter OAuth, or rotate unrelated credentials.
invariants: The new token remains valid throughout and after contraction.
The old token is revoked only after migration verification and audit evidence pass.
Only the identified old token is revoked.
No OAuth or unrelated credential is changed.
success_criteria: The old token fails authentication after revocation.
The new token continues to authenticate successfully.
All previously verified in-scope clients remain operational.
The final audit record contains the revocation event and outcome.
validation_steps: Confirm the contraction-ready audit result from bead-3.
Revoke the identified old token.
Attempt an authenticated request with the old token and confirm rejection.
Attempt representative requests with the new token and confirm success.
Check client health and append the revocation result to the audit record.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop before revocation if any client verification or audit prerequisite is missing; escalate if the old token remains accepted, the new token fails, or revocation affects any token other than the identified old token.

output.parent: Deliver backward-compatible API-token rotation through expansion, verified client migration, audit evidence, and contraction.

requirements.statement: Expand token validity so the old and new API tokens are simultaneously accepted during rotation.
Migrate all in-scope clients to the new API token and produce verifiable audit evidence of migration.
After migration verification, contract the validity window by revoking the old API token.

risks_or_open_decisions: OAuth is explicitly out of scope.
Do not revoke the old token until all in-scope client migrations are verified and audit evidence is retained.

childbeads.title: Expand API-token validity for backward-compatible rotation
childbeads.commit_sentence: Expand API-token rotation to accept both old and new tokens.
childbeads.outcome: The new token is active while the old token remains valid, allowing clients to migrate without an availability break.
context_refs: Completed Wayfinder decision map: backward-compatible API-token rotation
Resolved decision: expansion requires two valid tokens
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Implement only the token expansion needed for API-token rotation. Provision or activate the new token, retain the old token as valid, and update focused tests or configuration required to prove simultaneous validity. OAuth changes are not allowed.
invariants: Both the old and new API tokens remain valid during the migration window.
Existing authenticated API clients remain backward compatible.
OAuth remains out of scope.
success_criteria: The new token authenticates successfully.
The old token continues to authenticate successfully during expansion.
Focused automated coverage proves simultaneous validity.
The repository remains coherent after this commit.
validation_steps: Run the focused token-authentication tests.
Exercise authentication once with the old token and once with the new token.
Confirm no OAuth configuration or implementation changed.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop if the token store, authentication path, or rotation interface cannot support two concurrently valid tokens without an unresolved design decision.

childbeads.title: Migrate and verify all in-scope API clients
childbeads.commit_sentence: Migrate in-scope clients to the new API token with audit evidence.
childbeads.outcome: Every in-scope client uses the new token, and migration completion is independently verifiable before contraction.
context_refs: Completed Wayfinder decision map: backward-compatible API-token rotation
Resolved decision: client migration must be verified and audit evidence retained
cb-1: Expand API-token validity for backward-compatible rotation
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Update only in-scope API clients and their token references to use the new token. Verify client authentication and record audit evidence identifying each migrated client and its verification result. Do not revoke the old token or change OAuth behavior.
invariants: The old token remains valid while migration verification is incomplete.
Every in-scope client has a recorded migration and verification result.
OAuth remains out of scope.
success_criteria: All in-scope clients are configured with the new token.
Each client successfully authenticates using the new token.
Audit evidence is retained and identifies the verified clients.
No client remains dependent on the old token for normal operation.
validation_steps: Enumerate the authoritative in-scope client list.
Verify each client authenticates with the new token.
Check the audit evidence for complete client coverage and successful results.
Confirm the old token has not been revoked in this commit.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop if the authoritative client inventory is unavailable, any client cannot be verified with the new token, or audit evidence cannot prove complete migration.

childbeads.title: Contract rotation by revoking the old API token
childbeads.commit_sentence: Revoke the old API token after verified client migration.
childbeads.outcome: The old token is revoked only after complete migration verification, completing the token rotation and closing the compatibility window.
context_refs: Completed Wayfinder decision map: backward-compatible API-token rotation
Resolved decision: contraction revokes the old token after verified migration
cb-2: Migrate and verify all in-scope API clients
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Review the migration verification and audit evidence from the preceding slice, then revoke the old API token. Add focused proof that the old token fails while the new token continues to authenticate. Do not alter client scope or introduce OAuth changes.
invariants: Revocation occurs only after complete migration verification and retained audit evidence.
The new API token remains valid after contraction.
OAuth remains out of scope.
success_criteria: The old API token is revoked.
Requests using the old token are rejected.
Requests using the new token continue to succeed.
The migration audit evidence remains available as proof of the prerequisite.
validation_steps: Confirm the migration audit evidence covers every in-scope client.
Revoke the old token through the existing supported rotation mechanism.
Verify the old token is rejected.
Verify the new token still authenticates successfully.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop before revocation if migration evidence is incomplete, any client still requires the old token, or the new token fails validation.

Exercise missing-verification and missing-audit scenarios and verify the old token remains valid.
Confirm OAuth-related behavior and files are unchanged.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop if verified migration or audit evidence cannot be established, if revocation could affect the replacement token, or if the change would require OAuth behavior.

verified_runtime.evidence: accepted CLI invocation trace with completed usage event

output.parent: Outcome: perform a backward-compatible API-token rotation with two valid tokens during migration, verified client migration, audit evidence, and final revocation of the predecessor token. Non-goal: OAuth.
source_refs: Supplied completed Wayfinder decision map: backward-compatible API-token rotation
Supplied closed decisions: dual-token expansion, verified client migration, audit evidence, and predecessor-token revocation

requirements.statement: Expand rotation support so the predecessor and replacement API tokens are concurrently valid during migration.
Migrate every in-scope client to the replacement token and retain verifiable audit evidence of migration.
Contract the rotation window by revoking the predecessor token only after migration verification and audit evidence are complete.

risks_or_open_decisions: Use the accepted spec's authoritative token store, in-scope client inventory, telemetry, and audit sink; do not infer missing systems or clients.
Stop before contraction if any client remains unverified, uses the predecessor token, or lacks audit evidence.

childbeads.title: Expand API-token rotation to two concurrently valid tokens
childbeads.commit_sentence: Support backward-compatible rotation with both predecessor and replacement API tokens valid.
childbeads.outcome: The replacement token can be introduced without interrupting clients that still use the predecessor token.
context_refs: Supplied completed Wayfinder decision map: backward-compatible API-token rotation
Accepted spec's token-store and authentication-path definitions
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Implement the rotation expansion in the authoritative token store and authentication path named by the accepted spec. Create or register the replacement token, retain the predecessor token, and update focused tests or configuration required to prove concurrent validity. Do not revoke the predecessor token, migrate clients, add OAuth support, or change unrelated authentication behavior.
invariants: Exactly two API tokens are valid during the migration window.
Both predecessor and replacement tokens authenticate successfully for the existing API contract.
Unknown, malformed, or otherwise invalid tokens remain rejected.
OAuth remains out of scope.
The predecessor token is not revoked in this childbead.
success_criteria: The replacement token is registered and accepted by the existing API authentication path.
The predecessor token remains accepted during migration.
Focused authentication and regression coverage proves both-token validity and invalid-token rejection.
The repository remains coherent with no contraction or client-migration behavior included.
validation_steps: Run the accepted spec's prescribed focused token-authentication tests.
Exercise an existing representative API request with each valid token and verify successful authentication.
Exercise the same authentication path with an invalid token and verify rejection.
Confirm the predecessor token remains enabled after validation.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop and escalate if the accepted spec does not identify the authoritative token store or authentication path, if more than two tokens would become valid, or if implementing expansion requires an OAuth decision.

childbeads.title: Migrate and verify all in-scope clients
childbeads.commit_sentence: Migrate all in-scope clients to the replacement API token and verify their usage.
childbeads.outcome: Every client identified by the accepted spec uses the replacement token successfully while the predecessor token remains available as a controlled fallback.
context_refs: Supplied completed Wayfinder decision map: backward-compatible API-token rotation
Accepted spec's client inventory and migration procedure
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Using the accepted spec's authoritative client inventory, update each in-scope client's token reference to the replacement token. Execute the specified client verification and telemetry checks, documenting client identity, replacement-token success, predecessor-token non-use, timestamp, and result in the authoritative audit sink. Do not revoke the predecessor token or modify out-of-scope clients.
invariants: Every in-scope client is accounted for individually.
The predecessor token remains valid until verification and evidence are complete.
Client migration does not alter API behavior or introduce downtime.
Token secret values are not written to logs or audit records.
OAuth remains out of scope.
success_criteria: All in-scope clients authenticate successfully with the replacement token.
Verification shows no remaining predecessor-token use by an in-scope client.
Audit evidence is complete, attributable to each client, and stored in the authoritative sink named by the accepted spec.
No client is marked migrated based only on configuration change without successful verification.
validation_steps: Run the accepted spec's client-migration and smoke-test commands for every in-scope client.
Review authentication telemetry or the specified verification signal to confirm replacement-token use and predecessor-token non-use for each client.
Review the resulting audit records for client identity, token identity, verification time, and pass/fail result.
Re-run the focused API authentication checks from rotation-expand to confirm both tokens remain valid before contraction.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop and escalate if the client inventory, verification signal, or authoritative audit sink is incomplete; if any client fails migration; or if predecessor-token use remains detectable.

childbeads.title: Revoke the predecessor API token after verified migration
childbeads.commit_sentence: Revoke the predecessor API token after all client migrations are verified and audited.
childbeads.outcome: The rotation is contracted safely: the predecessor token is rejected and the replacement token remains the sole valid API token.
context_refs: Supplied completed Wayfinder decision map: backward-compatible API-token rotation
Accepted spec's contraction and rollback procedure
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Review the completed migration verification and audit evidence, then revoke only the predecessor token through the authoritative token-management mechanism. Retain the replacement token and run post-revocation authentication checks. Do not revoke or rotate the replacement token, alter client scope, or add OAuth behavior.
invariants: Revocation occurs only after every in-scope client has passed verification and audit evidence is complete.
The replacement token remains valid after contraction.
The predecessor token is rejected after revocation.
No token secret values are exposed in logs or audit records.
OAuth remains out of scope.
success_criteria: The predecessor token is revoked through the authoritative mechanism.
Requests using the predecessor token are rejected.
Requests using the replacement token continue to succeed.
Post-contraction regression checks pass and the revocation event is recorded as required by the accepted spec.
validation_steps: Review rotation-migrate-verify's complete client verification and audit evidence before executing revocation.
Run the accepted spec's contraction command or procedure against the predecessor token.
Verify a representative API request with the predecessor token is rejected.
Verify representative API requests with the replacement token succeed.
Run the accepted spec's post-rotation regression and audit checks.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Do not revoke if any client lacks successful verification or audit evidence, if predecessor-token use is still observed, or if the replacement token fails pre-contraction checks; escalate with the failed evidence and follow the accepted spec's rollback procedure.

output.parent: direct-accepted-spec

requirements.statement: Users can export one report as CSV and validate the downloaded header.

childbeads.title: Export one report as CSV with a validated download header
childbeads.commit_sentence: Add CSV export for one report and validate its downloaded header.
childbeads.outcome: Users can trigger a CSV export for the specified report and receive a download whose header is validated against the report's expected columns.
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Implement only the specified report's user-triggered CSV export, download response, header validation, and focused automated coverage. Reuse existing report data, column definitions, and application conventions. Do not add other report formats, bulk export, or unrelated UI/API changes.
invariants: The export represents the selected report.
The CSV header preserves the report's expected column names and order.
Header validation fails clearly when the downloaded header does not match the expected header.
success_criteria: A user can invoke the specified report's CSV export.
The downloaded file is valid CSV and contains the expected header.
Automated tests verify the export and exact downloaded-header validation.
validation_steps: Run the focused automated tests covering the report CSV export and downloaded header.
Verify the downloaded CSV header matches the report's expected columns and order.
Verify a mismatched header is rejected by the validation path.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop and escalate if the specified report or its expected header cannot be identified from the accepted spec and existing report definition, or if implementing the export requires unrelated schema, authentication, or cross-report changes.

output.parent: Implement import/export validation gates and publish-after-both workflow

requirements.statement: Validate imports.
Validate exports.
Publish only after both import and export validations succeed.

childbeads.title: Add import validation
childbeads.commit_sentence: Add validation that verifies imports before downstream publication.
childbeads.outcome: Imports are validated as an independently verifiable behavior.
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Implement the import-validation behavior and its directly coupled tests. Do not modify export validation or publication gating except where a shared validation interface is required.
invariants: Import validation remains independently executable.
A failed import validation is observable as a validation failure.
No publication behavior is introduced in this slice.
success_criteria: Import validation exists and correctly accepts valid imports.
Invalid imports fail validation.
Tests cover successful and failed import validation.
validation_steps: Run the focused import-validation test suite.
Run the relevant static checks for changed code.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop if the import contract or validation failure semantics are unspecified by the implementation context; escalate the missing contract instead of inventing behavior.

childbeads.title: Add export validation
childbeads.commit_sentence: Add validation that verifies exports before downstream publication.
childbeads.outcome: Exports are validated as an independently verifiable behavior.
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Implement the export-validation behavior and its directly coupled tests. Do not modify import validation or publication gating except where a shared validation interface is required.
invariants: Export validation remains independently executable.
A failed export validation is observable as a validation failure.
No publication behavior is introduced in this slice.
success_criteria: Export validation exists and correctly accepts valid exports.
Invalid exports fail validation.
Tests cover successful and failed export validation.
validation_steps: Run the focused export-validation test suite.
Run the relevant static checks for changed code.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop if the export contract or validation failure semantics are unspecified by the implementation context; escalate the missing contract instead of inventing behavior.

childbeads.title: Gate publication on both validations
childbeads.commit_sentence: Publish only after import and export validation both succeed.
childbeads.outcome: Publication is prevented unless both import and export validations have succeeded.
childbeads.scope_and_allowed_actions: Implement the publication gate that consumes the existing import and export validation results, plus directly coupled tests. Do not redesign either validation implementation or add unrelated publication behavior.
invariants: Publication cannot occur when import validation fails.
Publication cannot occur when export validation fails.
Publication occurs only when both validations succeed.
Both validation results are evaluated before publication.
success_criteria: Publication succeeds when both validations pass.
Publication is blocked when import validation fails.
Publication is blocked when export validation fails.
Tests cover all three outcomes.
validation_steps: Run the focused publication-gating test suite.
Run the combined import, export, and publication validation tests.
Run the relevant static checks for changed code.
childbeads.stop_or_escalate_when: Stop if validation results cannot be consumed reliably from the preceding slices or if publication has additional undocumented prerequisites; escalate the contract gap instead of inventing prerequisites.
